research_answer: Namibia's Economic Complexity Index (ECI) ranks among the lowest of its regional and international peers, and this has been a persistent feature for at least two decades — not a recent development. Among its peer group, only Angola ranks lower than Namibia in economic complexity. This consistently low ECI is rooted in structural features of Namibia's export basket: it is composed mostly of primary products, has low diversity, and the products it does export tend to have high ubiquity (meaning many other countries also export them). Notably, no product in Namibia's current export basket has a Product Complexity Index (PCI) above the global median, and the sectors that account for most of the country's export diversity — Agriculture and Mineral products — are themselves low-complexity sectors.
supporting_quotes: Namibia's ECI is amongst the lowest of its regional and international peers, with an export basket composed mostly of primary products.
Low ECI has been a constant for the previous two decades, surpassing only Angola among the group of regional and international peers.

no product in its current export basket displays an average PCI above the global median, and the products that concentrate most of the country's diversity – Agriculture and Mineral products – tend to be of low complexity.

data_points.metric: Products with PCI above global median
